---
title: "Appendix, Glossary, and Bibliography"
---

::: {.archive-notice}
**Source:** Pages 309--322 of *MintonThesis.pdf* (September 2009). Text extracted from PDF.
:::

## Appendix: The Screening Tool

As a result of a request for information I made, which was initially rejected, then only acted upon when I responded by mentioning, explicitly, the Freedom of Information Act, I received the following information about how the Screening Tool works on 13 May 2008:

> A score will be produced based on the responses to the screening questions. The higher the score, the more likely it is that the claimant will return to work within a year from the start of their Incapacity Benefit claim. If the score is above the threshold value of 1.11, then the claimant will be screened out and not required to attend any additional WFI's. However, if the score is below the threshold they will still be required to attend additional WFI's.
>
> The variables that are important in predicting whether or not a claimant should be screened in or out are each given a parameter value. The score will be obtained by giving the claimants' response a value; 1 for a positive response or 0 for a negative response. This is then multiplied by the appropriate parameter and totalled to give the final score.
>
> For example, a person who expects to work within 3 months and their last job was 'clerical and secretarial', but who did not match any of the other entries in the table, would have a score of 1.9514 (0.7599 + 1.1915). As this is greater than the 1.11 threshold, this claimant would not be required to attend further WFI's.

The response also included a copy of the document *Incapacity Benefit Algorithm Definition*, which included the following table:

: The Screening Tool {#tbl-a1}

| Question Number & Text | Response Text (or Notes Explaining) | Score |
|---|---|---|
| 3 Date of Birth / 4 Are you: | Applicant being older than 50 and younger than 61 and male (Age >= 51 and Age < 60) | -0.5283 |
| 7 Do you live with a partner? | Live on your own | -0.3249 |
| 9 When did you last have a job? | More than 24 months ago | -1.7989 |
| 11 When do you see yourself working (again)? | Within the next 3 months | +0.7599 |
| 11 When do you see yourself working (again)? | Between 3 and 6 months time | +1.1005 |
| 12a Do you have any formal qualifications? | No | -0.4339 |
| 17 ...would you estimate your income was: | Last job paid less than £200 per week | -0.5075 |
| 17 ...would you estimate your income was: | Less than £10,000 pa | -0.5075 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Manager and senior officials | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Professional Occupations | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Associate professional and technical occupations | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Administrative and secretarial occupations | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Skilled trades occupations | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Personal service occupations | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Sales and customer service occupations | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Process, Plant and machine operatives | +1.1915 |
| 13 Would you describe your last regular job as: | Elementary Occupations | +1.1915 |
| 14 What is the main health problem/disability...? | Diseases of the Skin and Subcutaneous Tissue (L00-L99) (applied once only) | -1.3048 |
| 14 What is the main health problem/disability...? | Diseases of the Nervous System (G00-G99) (applied once only) | -1.7724 |

The 'Appendix A' referenced above defines the following as 'Diseases of the Nervous System':

> Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, axonic brain injury, arachnoiditis, Bell's Palsy or paralysis, brain damage, carpal tunnel syndrome, cataplexy, cerebellar ataxia, cerebral abscess, cerebral palsy, chorea, demyelination, disseminated sclerosis, dystonia, encephalitis, epilepsy, facial paralysis, grand mal, Guillain-Barre syndrome, hemiparesis, hemiplegia, Huntington's chorea, hydrocephalus, insomnia, meningitis, migraine, motor-neurone disease, multiple sclerosis, muscular dystrophy, myalgic encephalomyelitis, myasthenia, myasthenia gravis, myelitis, narcolepsy, neuropathy, paralysis, paraplegia, Parkinson's disease or syndrome, parkinsonism, peripheral neuropathy, petit mal, phrenicectomy, polyneuritis, post viral fatigue syndrome, quadriplegia, spasmodic torticollis, spinal arachnoiditis, sympathectomy, tetraplegia, tic douloureux, transient ischaemic attack, transverse myelitis, trigeminal neuralgia, viral fatigue

And the following as 'Diseases of the Skin and Subcutaneous Tissues':

> Abscess, acne, boils, carbuncle, cellulitis, cheiropompholyx, connective tissue disorder, cyst septic, cyst, pilonidal, dermatitis of any kind, eczema, erythema, erythema nodosum, fistula, furuncle(s), furunculosis, impetigo, ingrowing nails, leg ulcer, mixed connective tissue disorder, paronychia, pruritus, psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, psoriatic arthropathy, sebaceous cyst, septic cyst, skin disease, skin eruption, skin lesion, sunburn, ulcer(s) other, urticaria, whitlow

## Glossary

| Abbreviation | Meaning |
|---|---|
| ADF | Advisors Discretionary Fund |
| AR | Authority Ranking |
| CDS | Corporate Document Services |
| CMP | Condition Management Programme |
| CS | Communal Sharing |
| DiD | Difference in Differences |
| DWP | Department for Work and Pensions |
| EM | Equality Matching |
| ESA | Employment and Support Allowance |
| IB | Incapacity Benefit |
| IBPA | Incapacity Benefits Personal Advisor |
| ILO | International Labour Organisation |
| IVB | Invalidity Benefit |
| IWS | In Work Support |
| LFS | Labour Force Survey |
| LLTI | Limiting Longstanding Illness |
| MP | Market Pricing |
| NDDP | New Deal for Disabled People |
| NI | National Insurance |
| NNH | Number Needed to Harm |
| NNO | Number Needed to Observe |
| NNT | Number Needed to Treat |
| OECD | Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development |
| ONS | Office for National Statistics |
| RMT | Relational Models Theory |
| RTWC | Return to Work Credit |
| SDA | Severe Disablement Allowance |
| SIC | Standard Industrial Classifications |
| SOC | Standard Occupational Classifications |
| TSO | The Stationery Office |
| WFI | Work Focussed Interview |

## Bibliography

*The full bibliography for this thesis is extensive and spans pages 314--322 of the original PDF. Key references cited in chapters 10--12 include:*

- Adam, S., C. Emmerson, C. Frayne and A. Goodman (2006). *Early quantitative evidence on the impact of the Pathways to Work pilots.* DWP, Corporate Document Services.
- Adam, S., A. Bozio, C. Emmerson, D. Greenberg and G. Knight (2008). *A cost-benefit analysis of Pathways to Work for new and repeat incapacity benefits claimants.* DWP. Norwich, HMSO.
- Altman, D. G. (1998). "Confidence intervals for the number needed to treat." *British Medical Journal* 317: 1309--12.
- Aylward, M. and G. Waddell (2005). *The Scientific and Conceptual Basis of Incapacity Benefit.* London, The Stationary Office.
- Beatty, C. and S. Fothergill (1996). "Labour market adjustment in areas of chronic industrial decline: The case of the UK coalfields." *Regional Studies* 30(7): 627--640.
- Beatty, C., S. Fothergill and R. Macmillan (2000). "A Theory of Employment, Unemployment and Sickness." *Regional Studies* 34(7): 617--630.
- Bewley, H., R. Dorsett and G. Haile (2007). *The Impact of Pathways to Work.* DWP. London, Corporate Document Services.
- Bewley, H., R. Dorsett and M. Ratto (2008). *Evidence on the effect of Pathways to Work on existing claimants.* DWP. Norwich, HMSO.
- Black, D., J. Smith, M. Berger and B. Noel (2003). "Is the Threat of Reemployment Services More Effective than the Services Themselves? Experimental Evidence from the UI System." *American Economic Review* 93(4): 1313--1327.
- Blyth, B. (2006). *Incapacity Benefit reforms -- Pathways to Work Pilots performance and analysis.* DWP. Leeds, Corporate Document Services.
- Coleman, N. and L. Kennedy (2005). *Destination of benefit leavers 2004.* DWP. Leeds, Corporate Document Services.
- Cook, R. J. and D. L. Sackett (1995). "The number needed to treat: a clinically useful measure of treatment effect." *British Medical Journal* 310: 452--454.
- DWP (2002). *Pathways to work: Helping people into employment.* DWP. London, TSO.
- DWP (2006). *A New Deal for Welfare: Empowering people to work.* DWP, The Stationery Office.
- Gigerenzer, G. (2003). *Reckoning with Risk: Learning to Live with Uncertainty.* London, Penguin.
- Heckman, J. J. and V. J. Hotz (1989). "Choosing Among Alternative Nonexperimental Methods for Estimating the Impact of Social Programs." *Journal of the American Statistical Association* 84(408): 862--874.
- Loehlin, J. C. (1998). *Latent Variable Models: An Introduction to Factor, Path, and Structural Analysis.* Mahwah, N.J., Lawrence Erlbaum Associates.
- Merton, R. K. (1968). *Social Theory and Social Structure.* New York, Free Press.
- Taleb, N. N. (2007). *The Black Swan: The Impact of the Highly Improbable.* London, Penguin.
- Webster, D. (2006). "Welfare Reform: Facing up to the Geography of Worklessness." *Local Economy* 21(2): 107--116.
- Wilkinson, R. (2005). *The Impact of Inequality: How to make sick societies healthier.* Oxford, Routledge.
- Wooldridge, J. M. (2006). *Introductory Econometrics: A Modern Approach.* Mason, OH, Thompson South-Western.
